lessons.title: Java Introduction
lessons.content:
What is Java?

  • Java is a popular programming language, created in 1995.
  • It is owned by Oracle, and more than 3 billion devices run Java.
  • It is used for: Mobile applications (Android), Desktop applications, Web applications, Games, and much more.

lessons.title: Java Get Started
lessons.content:
Quickstart

In Java, every application begins with a class name, and that class must match the filename.

public class Main {   public static void main(String[] args) {     System.out.println("Hello World");   } }

lessons.title: Java Syntax
lessons.content:
Java Syntax

The code in a Java file is usually wrapped in a class. A class is a container for code that represents an object.

The main() method is required and you will see it in every Java program.

Any code inside the main() method will be executed.

lessons.title: Java Output
lessons.content:
Print Text

You can use the println() method to print a line of text to the screen:

System.out.println("Hello World!");

There is also a print() method, which is similar to println(), but it does not insert a new line at the end of the output.

lessons.title: Java Comments
lessons.content:
Comments

Single-line comments start with two forward slashes (//).

Multi-line comments start with /* and end with */.

lessons.title: Java Variables
lessons.content:
Variables

Variables are containers for storing data values.

  • String - stores text, such as "Hello"
  • int - stores integers (whole numbers)
  • float - stores floating point numbers
  • char - stores single characters
  • boolean - stores values with two states: true or false
